description_long: This object consists of two adjacent stone obelisks set into a patch of grassy earth. The left obelisk is taller, with a weathered, pale stone surface and a simple, slightly pointed tip. The right obelisk is shorter, darker, and features a ribbed texture with visible weathering and rust-like stains, topped by a pyramidal cap. The right obelisk also displays an inscription on its front face, partially legible as 'S.P. 1881'. Both obelisks are embedded in a rectangular base of earth and grass, suggesting they are fixed monuments. The surfaces are rough and aged, with visible signs of erosion and lichen growth. The overall appearance is that of historical or boundary markers, commonly found in parks, roadsides, or urban green spaces.
description: Two weathered stone obelisks, one ribbed and inscribed, set in a grassy earth base. The surfaces are rough, aged, and show signs of erosion and lichen.
description_view_0: Front view showing both obelisks side by side, highlighting the height difference and the ribbed texture of the right obelisk.
description_view_1: View focused on the inscribed face of the right obelisk, with the inscription 'S.P. 1881' clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear angled view showing the plain, weathered back surfaces of both obelisks and the grassy base.
description_view_3: View of the back of the left obelisk, with faint, eroded markings and a clear view of the grassy base.
attribute_inscription: The right obelisk has an inscription on its front face, reading 'S.P. 1881' in uppercase, serifed letters. The text is weathered but still legible, carved into the stone.
attribute_ornamentation: None visible beyond the ribbed texture on the right obelisk.
attribute_base_design: The base is a rectangular patch of earth and grass, slightly raised and uneven, with no decorative features or steps.
attribute_tip_design: Both obelisks have simple, pointed tips; the left is more conical, while the right has a small pyramidal cap.
attribute_surrounding_features: The obelisks are surrounded by a patch of grass and soil; no other features are present.
